Title: High-spatiotemporal-resolution transcriptomes provide insights into fruit development and ripening in sinensis

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter; ABF2-responsive promoter context under ABA signalling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for improving citrus fruit sugar accumulation in juice sacs; requires direct functional transgenics/editing for deployment
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely plasma membrane/apoplasmic interface; expression detected in juice sac epidermal cells
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fengjie 72-1; correlation also tested in Anliu/Honganliu and other citrus varieties
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Further functional work needed; altering apoplasmic sucrose efflux may affect source-sink partitioning
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWEET15 was specifically highly expressed in segment membrane and juice sac, with increasing expression during fruit development. 2. SWEET15 expression positively correlated with fruit sugar content across multiple citrus varieties. 3. FISH localized SWEET15 transcripts to juice sac epidermal cells. 4. ABF2 can promote/interact with the SWEET15 promoter, linking ABA signalling to sucrose accumulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO GSE125726; comparative RNA-seq from SRA SRP070469
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in SM and JS; increases with fruit development and ripening
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sucrose accumulation in citrus fruit juice sacs; proposed to mediate sucrose release into the apoplasm
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; ABA analogue AM1 up-regulates expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies SWEET15 as a key sugar accumulation gene, but direct gain/loss-of-function in citrus was not performed here.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq spatiotemporal profiling; correlation across varieties; fluorescence in situ hybridization; AM1 qRT-PCR; Y1H/dual-luciferase with ABF2
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major determinant of sweetness and fruit quality
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in segment membrane and juice sac, increasing with development

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering fruit acidity via vacuolar acidification/citrate storage
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuolar proton pump / tonoplast-associated acidification system
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fengjie 72-1; expression compared across Succari, Bingtang, Xinhui and Newhall
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing acidity may alter palatability and broad vacuolar pH-dependent metabolism
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsPH8 was specifically expressed in segment membrane and juice sac and was highly expressed in juice sac. 2. CsPH8 expression positively correlated with citric acid content among citrus varieties with different acidity. 3. The study proposes CsPH8 as a key gene for citrate transport/storage in juice sac vacuoles and therefore citrate accumulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO GSE125726; comparative RNA-seq from SRA SRP070469
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically high in SM and especially JS; increases with development
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes citrate accumulation/acidity, likely by energizing vacuolar citrate uptake/storage
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; natural varietal acidity differences
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior overexpression evidence from other studies, but within this paper support is transcriptomic correlation and network inference.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq spatiotemporal profiling; correlation across citrus varieties
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines fruit acidity and taste quality
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically high in segment membrane and juice sac, especially juice sac

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.1 was dramatically up-regulated after 155 DAF in all four fruit tissues while free IAA declined. 2. Exogenous ABA analogue AM1 strongly induced GH3.1 expression. 3. ABF2 interacted with/promoted the GH3.1 promoter, supporting GH3.1 as a mediator of ABA-controlled auxin homeostasis during ripening.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux away from free target product by conjugation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated after 155 DAF in EP, AL, SM and JS during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase family
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes reduction of free IAA during citrus fruit ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong induction after 155 DAF in all four fruit tissues; induced by AM1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin homeostasis during fruit development and ripening
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates free IAA to amino acids, reducing active auxin levels
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in citrus; AM1 treatment used, constitutive promoter not applicable
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development stage; exogenous ABA analogue AM1
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for auxin homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR after AM1 treatment; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO GSE125726; comparative RNA-seq from SRA SRP070469
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames GH3.1 as an auxin-homeostasis gene downstream of ABA/ABF2 rather than a primary sugar/acid enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced IAA may affect growth-related processes during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate auxin decline and coordinate ripening with sugar/color development

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABF2 expression tracked ABA accumulation during fruit development. 2. AM1 treatment strongly induced ABF2 and promoted colouring, chlorophyll degradation and sugar accumulation. 3. ABF2 directly interacted with promoters of genes involved in sucrose transport, chlorophyll degradation, auxin homeostasis, carotenoid and ABA biosynthesis, and cell wall metabolism. 4. The study proposes ABF2 as a master regulator of ABA signalling during citrus fruit ripening.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sucrose transport; carotenoid and ABA biosynthesis; chlorophyll degradation; auxin homeostasis; cell wall metabolism during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO GSE125726; comparative RNA-seq from SRA SRP070469
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Increasing with fruit development in accordance with ABA levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ripening-associated sugar accumulation, chlorophyll degradation, carotenoid/ABA pathway activation, and auxin decline
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq coexpression analysis; AM1 treatment with qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid assay; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Juice sac for sugars; peel/pulp for ripening-associated pigments and hormones
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; exogenous ABA analogue AM1
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Fengjie 72-1; AM1 validation in Satsuma mandarin
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sugar accumulation affects fruit quality; carotenoids/ABA/chlorophyll turnover influence ripening and coloration; auxin homeostasis coordinates development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or promotes PSY; Z-ISO; NCED1/2/4; HCAR; PAO; RCCR; PPH; NYC1; SUT4; SWEET10; SWEET15; SWEET16; GH3.1; Expansin-A1/A8
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters of target genes containing ABRE cis-elements; AM1-inducible context
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PSY promoter; Z-ISO promoter; NCED1 promoter; NCED2 promoter; NCED4 promoter; HCAR promoter; PAO promoter; RCCR promoter; PPH promoter; NYC1 promoter; SUT4 promoter; SWEET10 promoter; SWEET15 promoter; SWEET16 promoter; GH3.1 promoter; Expansin-A1 promoter; Expansin-A8 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bZIP transcription factor, ABRE-binding factor family
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate master regulator for ripening engineering to improve sugar accumulation and peel coloration; should be used carefully due to broad pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator and magnifier of ABA signalling controlling multiple metabolic branches during fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SWEET15; SWEET10; SUT4; GH3.1; PSY; Z-ISO; NCED1; NCED2; NCED4; HCAR; PAO; RCCR; PPH; NYC1; Expansin-A1; Expansin-A8
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad control over multiple pathways may cause pleiotropic changes in growth, softening and hormone balance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rises with development in citrus fruit tissues; induced by AM1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely positive feedback via activation of NCED genes and ABA biosynthesis; also links ABA to carotenoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly calls ABF2 the key last gene in the ABA signalling module and proposes it as master regulator rather than merely a correlated TF.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB5 was identified as a highly connected hub TF in tissue-specific coexpression networks. 2. MYB5 was highly coexpressed with CsPH8, a key citrate-associated proton pump. 3. The study proposes MYB5 as a candidate regulator of CsPH8 and citrate accumulation, but no direct binding assay was shown.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Putative regulation of vacuolar citrate transport/storage through CsPH8 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as hub TF; highly coexpressed with CsPH8 in citrus fruit tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely positive regulator of citrate accumulation via association with CsPH8 expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Citric acid determines fruit acidity and taste quality
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for acidity engineering, pending direct validation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent candidate controller, not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with CsPH8 during fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is coexpression only; included because the paper highlights MYB5 among TFs potentially regulating CsPH8.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 was a highly connected hub TF in coexpression networks. 2. TT8 was highly coexpressed with CsPH8. 3. The study proposes TT8 as a candidate regulator contributing to citrate accumulation through CsPH8 regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Putative regulation of vacuolar citrate transport/storage through CsPH8 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hub TF; highly coexpressed with CsPH8
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely positive regulator of citrate accumulation via association with CsPH8
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for acidity manipulation with further validation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent candidate controller, not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible MYB-bHLH regulatory context, not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct regulatory evidence on CsPH8 was not provided in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPC was highly coexpressed with CsPH8. 2. The study proposes CPC as a candidate regulator of CsPH8 and citrate accumulation in citrus fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Putative regulation of vacuolar citrate transport/storage through CsPH8 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate regulator potentially promoting citrate accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate acidity regulator requiring direct validation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only coexpression support in this paper.
